title: Workhorse Group Reports Full Year 2024 Results
text: Workhorse Group reported a net loss of $101.8 million for 2024, compared to $123.9 million in 2023. Sales declined to $6.6 million from $13.1 million year-over-year, primarily due to lower W4 CC truck sales partially offset by increased W56 sales and service revenue. The company secured regulatory approval in Canada for its W4 CC and W56 models. Management noted expectations to begin deploying demo trucks to Canada in April 2025. Workhorse expanded its product line by launching the W56 208-inch wheelbase model with 1,200 cubic feet of cargo capacity, a 20% increase from the standard version. Recent accomplishments include a GSA contract, sales to the City of Tacoma, and a California state contract. The company demonstrated fleet electrification through its Stables by Workhorse initiative, delivering nearly 154,000 packages using 13 electric vehicles during the 2024 peak season. As of December 31, 2024, Workhorse held $4.6 million in cash. The company secured $35 million in convertible financing in February 2025.
